workflow.name: Generate UGC product videos from Google Sheets with DALL·E, GPT-4 and Sora
workflow.description:
## This n8n template demonstrates how to fully automate the creation of UGC-style product videos using AI, starting from a simple Google Sheet.

It transforms product data into AI-generated images, cinematic video scripts, and final videos, then uploads everything to Google Drive and updates your sheet automatically. 

## 💡 Use cases
- Generate UGC ads at scale for e-commerce products
- Create TikTok / Reels content automatically
- Build content pipelines for agencies or creators
- Rapidly test different product angles, audiences, and messaging
- Automate creative production from structured data (Google Sheets)


## Good to know

1. This workflow uses multiple AI services (image + video), so cost depends on usage:
- Image generation (DALL·E)
- Video generation (Sora)

2. Video generation is asynchronous and may take several minutes per item
3. Some AI models (like Sora) may be region-restricted or limited access
4. Generated image URLs may expire, so storing them (as done here) is important

## How it works
1. Reads product data from Google Sheets and selects rows marked "Pending".
2. Creates a prompt and generates a product image.
3. Analyzes the image and turns it into a video script.
4. Sends the script to Sora and waits until the video is ready.
5. Uploads the video to Google Drive and updates the sheet.
6. Logs errors and marks the row as "Error".

## How to use

1. Add products to Google Sheets with name, description, audience, and set status to "Pending".
2. Run the workflow or let the schedule trigger process items automatically.
3. The system generates image → script → video, uploads them, and updates your sheet.

## Requirements

1. Requires OpenAI, Google Sheets, and Google Drive accounts.
2. Requires an n8n instance with credentials configured.


## Customizing this workflow

1. Replace the schedule trigger with a webhook or form for real-time use.
2. Generate multiple videos per product.
3. Send outputs to platforms like TikTok, Meta Ads, or CMS tools.
4. Add voiceovers, captions, or permanent asset storage.

workflow.name: Automated UGC Video Generator from Google Sheets

parameters.content:
## ERROR HANDLER WORKFLOW
This workflow is triggered when the main UGC pipeline fails.
It extracts the error details and row number, then updates
the Google Sheet with Status = "Error" and the error message.

SETUP: In the main workflow settings, set this workflow
as the "Error Workflow".

## 5. GOOGLE DRIVE UPLOAD & SHEET UPDATE
Fetches the video binary from Sora API.
Uploads MP4 to Google Drive.
Sets file to public (anyone with link).
Builds shareable Drive URL.
Writes Image URL + Video URL back to the
correct row. Sets Status = "Done".
Loops back for next pending product.
On error, the Error Handler workflow sets
Status = "Error" + error message.

## 4b. SORA POLLING LOOP
Polls GET /v1/videos/{videoId} every 30s. Check Sora Status merges poll response with
carried product data from Extract Sora Job ID.

IF node checks isDone === true:
  TRUE  -> Fetch video content + upload to Drive
  FALSE -> Loop back to Wait 30 Seconds
Max 40 polls = 20 minute timeout safety.

## 4. SORA VIDEO GENERATION (Async + Polling)
Waits 60s for rate limiting, then POSTs to Sora API. Sora returns a queued job ID (not a video URL).

Extracts the job ID, then enters a polling loop:
  - Wait 30s between polls
  - GET /v1/videos/{id} to check status
  - If status != "completed", loop back to wait
  - Max 40 polls (20 min timeout)
Once completed, fetches video content URL.

## 3. VISION ANALYSIS & VIDEO SCRIPT
GPT-4 Vision analyzes the generated image for mood, colors, composition. This enriches the video script with visual continuity. Script optimized for Sora's prompt format.

## 2. IMAGE GENERATION

For each pending product, a code node builds a detailed DALL·E 3 prompt with UGC aesthetic direction. The prompt is sent to the OpenAI API to generate an HD image, and the returned image URL is extracted for downstream use.

## Stage 1 — Trigger & data fetch. 

A schedule trigger fires on a set interval. It reads all rows from a Google Sheets "Products" tab, then a code node filters down to only rows where Status = "Pending", tracking each row number for later updates.

## Workflow overview

This is an n8n automation workflow that turns product listings in a Google Sheet into UGC-style videos, fully hands-off.

It runs on a schedule, picks up any row marked "Pending" in the sheet, and for each product it: generates a product image with DALL·E 3, analyzes that image with GPT-4 Vision to inform the video style, sends a tailored prompt to Sora to generate a short video, uploads the finished video to Google Drive, and writes the public links back to the sheet. The row's status gets updated to "Done" when complete, or "Error" with details if something fails along the way.

In short: you fill in product info in a spreadsheet, and the workflow automatically produces and delivers AI-generated marketing videos for each one.

## Setup instructions

Before running this workflow, configure the following:

1. **Google Sheets OAuth** — Connect your Google account so the workflow can read product rows and write back status updates, image URLs, and video URLs.
2. **Google Drive credentials** — Needed for uploading finished MP4 videos and setting them to public access.
3. **OpenAI API key** — Used for both DALL·E 3 image generation and GPT-4 Vision analysis. Set this in the HTTP Request nodes for those calls.
4. **Sora API key** — Used for video generation. Configure in the Sora Generate Video and Poll Sora Status HTTP Request nodes.
5. **Google Sheet setup** — Create a sheet named "Products" with columns for product info, Status, Image URL, Video URL, and Error Message. Mark rows you want processed with Status = "Pending".
6. **Google Drive folder** — Set the target folder ID in the Upload Video node where finished videos will be stored.
7. **Schedule interval** — Adjust the Schedule Trigger to your preferred frequency (e.g. every 15 minutes, hourly).
8. **Error handler workflow** — Import the error handler as a separate workflow and link it in this workflow's settings under "Error Workflow" so failed rows get logged back to the sheet.

data.details: In n8n, when a workflow execution fails, it can start another workflow. This second workflow can be any arbitrary workflow on your n8n instance. Use the Error Trigger node as your Trigger in the Error workflow.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
